title: aquarius horizontalis
intro: Aquarius horizontalis, also known as Water Hemlock, is a highly toxic and invasive plant species native to North America. Its medicinal properties are derived from the plant's roots, leaves, and seeds, but due to its toxicity, it is not commonly used in herbal medicine. However, some herbal preparations, such as tinctures and infusions, may be used under the guidance of a medical professional to treat certain health conditions, including fever and rheumatism. Despite its potential medicinal uses, the health benefits of Aquarius horizontalis are largely outweighed by its risks, and it should be handled with extreme caution due to its highly toxic properties.
